title: Connarus pentaphyllus
intro: Connarus pentaphyllus, also known as the five-leafed Connarus, is a tropical plant species in the family Connaraceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions such as fever, respiratory issues, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Connarus pentaphyllus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Connarus pentaphyllus is commonly used to make decoctions, infusions, and dried extracts, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, cough, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Connarus pentaphyllus belongs to the family Connaraceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the staff trees or staff vine. The Connaraceae family consists of about 200 species, primarily distributed in tropical regions of Asia, Africa, and Australia. The Connaraceae family is also known as the staff vine family due to the presence of a characteristic staff-shaped structure on the stems of some species. Connarus pentaphyllus is a species of staff tree native to tropical Asia.
